topic: The Lyceum Greenfield Architecture — Wednesday Feb 18
summary: Aligned on Lyceum greenfield stack, MVP scope (one curriculum track, cohort flow, progress dashboard), and ~$200/mo pricing with free-tier tripwire.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning everyone. Different kind of standup today — we're here to architect The Lyceum from scratch. Let me frame this clearly: this is a standalone greenfield project. Completely independent codebase, no connection to Lever, joshlevylabs.com, or Clawdbot Hub. Target user is R&D and manufacturing engineers at electronics companies. We already have tickets A-079 and A-082 for repo scaffolding and data model, plus A-080 and A-081 for brand positioning and pricing. The question today: are we aligned on stack, MVP scope, product vision, and pricing direction? Let's go around. Elon, you're up first — you own the architecture tickets.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: The stack in A-079 is right — Next.js 14 App Router, Supabase for auth and Postgres, Stripe for subscriptions. Simple, fast, and we can ship an MVP in weeks, not months. For engineers building electronics, the core value is structured technical curriculum — think cohort-based learning tracks for things like PCB design, signal integrity, embedded systems testing. The data model in A-082 needs to account for hands-on lab exercises with hardware-in-the-loop references, not just video lessons. I'd push for markdown-based lesson content stored in the repo itself, with Postgres tracking progress and cohort state. No CMS bloat. Keep it lean.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The product vision has to be razor sharp or this dies. Engineers don't want another Udemy with worse production value. The Lyceum should feel like joining an elite technical guild — curated curriculum, small cohorts, real peer interaction. The MVP is three things: one complete curriculum track, a cohort enrollment flow, and a progress dashboard. That's it. No community forum, no certificates, no marketplace. Ship one track that's so good engineers tell their colleagues about it. The free tier gives you the first module of any track. Premium unlocks the full curriculum and cohort access. Simple.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: I like the free-first-module approach — that's a classic freemium tripwire. A-081 is already tracking the pricing strategy doc. At roughly two hundred a month for premium, we're in the professional development budget sweet spot — engineers can expense it without VP approval at most companies. The conversion funnel is: land on content, sample the free module, hit the paywall at module two. I want to model a B2B team plan early in the pricing doc even if we don't launch with it. Enterprise seats at this price point could be a five-figure annual contract per team.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From a measurement and instrumentation perspective, this is where I get excited. The engineers we're targeting live in a world of test equipment, data acquisition, and validation. If the curriculum includes practical lab content — actual measurement setups, instrument programming, data analysis workflows — that's a massive differentiator. No one is doing structured, cohort-based education for test and measurement engineering. We should scope the first curriculum track around something concrete like automated test system design. That anchors the whole platform in real engineering value.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: A-080 is in progress for brand positioning. The name "Lyceum" is strong — it signals classical rigor, which resonates with serious engineers. The landing page needs to lead with the problem: engineers waste hundreds of hours piecing together fragmented knowledge from vendor docs, YouTube, and tribal knowledge. We position this as the structured path. For launch, I want one killer landing page, a waitlist with a lead magnet — maybe a free technical reference guide — and a content SEO play targeting long-tail engineering queries. That builds the top of funnel organically.

transcript.speaker: Marc Benioff
transcript.message: Two hundred a month is the right entry point but let me push on the growth architecture. We need to think about this as a platform from day one, even if we ship one track. The data model should support multiple content creators eventually — bring in industry experts as track authors and revenue-share with them. That's how you scale content without scaling headcount. Also, the B2B motion Dave mentioned is critical. I'd want the Stripe integration in A-079 to support team billing and seat management from the start, even if the UI comes later.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Let's do one round of cross-talk. Steve, you said no marketplace — but Marc's pitching a multi-author platform. Where's the line?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Marc's right about the long-term vision, but wrong about when it matters. If we design the data model to support multiple authors, fine — that's a schema decision, costs us nothing. But the MVP product experience must be single-voice, single-track, obsessively curated. The moment you open it to multiple creators before you've proven the format works, you get content quality variance and you're dead. Prove the model with one perfect track first.

transcript.speaker: Marc Benioff
transcript.message: Fair enough — I'm not saying launch with a marketplace. I'm saying don't paint yourself into a corner architecturally. Author ID on the content model, revenue share fields on subscriptions. Future-proofing, not feature creep.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Agreed. I'll extend A-082 to include an author reference on the lesson and track models, and a team billing flag on the subscription schema. Minimal additions, no UI work. James, what's your recommendation for the first track topic?

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Automated test system architecture — covers LabVIEW, Python-based instrument control, measurement uncertainty, and test strategy. It's universal across hardware companies and there's essentially zero structured education for it outside vendor training.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: That's our direction. Let me close this out. Priority one: Elon completes the repo scaffold in A-079 and extends A-082 with author and team billing fields. Priority two: Joshua needs to decide on the first curriculum track topic and commit to creating or commissioning that content — that's the make-or-break asset. Priority three: Alex finishes brand positioning in A-080 and Dave finalizes the pricing model in A-081 with the B2B team tier included. Steve, I want you to draft a one-page MVP spec — three features only, nothing more. We reconvene next week with a deployable skeleton and a content plan. Let's move.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Joshua, thank you — those three decisions lock in everything we need to move. First, automated test system architecture as the inaugural track is exactly what James and the team recommended, and it gives us a clear differentiator in a space with zero structured competition. Second, the co-authoring model is smart — you bring the vision and pedagogical structure, an industry expert brings credibility and domain depth, and neither of you carries the full content

actionItems.text: Extend A-082 data model to include author reference on lessons/tracks and team billing flag on subscriptions
Complete greenfield repo scaffold per A-079 with Next.js 14, Supabase auth, Postgres, Stripe integration
Decide first curriculum track topic and content authorship model
Draft one-page MVP product spec: curriculum track view, cohort enrollment flow, progress dashboard — three features only
Finalize brand positioning doc and landing page copy framework per A-080
Complete pricing and monetization strategy with B2B team tier added to A-081
Draft shortlist of 3 candidate curriculum tracks with market sizing and differentiation analysis
Review and approve MVP product spec once drafted
Design waitlist landing page with lead magnet (free technical reference guide)

topPriorities: Complete repo scaffold (A-079) and extend data model (A-082) with author + team billing fields
Joshua decides first curriculum track topic and commits to content creation plan
Finalize brand positioning (A-080) and pricing strategy with B2B tier (A-081)

ceoDecisions.question: What should the first curriculum track topic be?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Automated test system architecture (LabVIEW/Python instrument control, measurement strategy)
options: Automated test system architecture (LabVIEW/Python instrument control, measurement strategy)
PCB design and signal integrity fundamentals
Embedded systems validation and debug workflows
Let James Truchard draft a shortlist of 3 tracks with market sizing before deciding

ceoDecisions.question: Will you author the first track yourself, commission an external SME, or co-author with an industry expert?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Co-author with a recognized industry expert for credibility and shared workload
options: Author it yourself based on your engineering experience
Commission an external subject matter expert with editorial oversight
Co-author with a recognized industry expert for credibility and shared workload

ceoDecisions.question: Should we include B2B team billing in the MVP launch or defer it to v1.1?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Build the schema now but defer the UI to v1.1 (Marc/Dave's recommendation)
options: Include team billing in MVP — build the schema and basic UI now
Build the schema now but defer the UI to v1.1 (Marc/Dave's recommendation)
Defer entirely — pure individual subscriptions for MVP
